div.p: The optimization exploits the term structure of Raft logs:

ul.li: A divergence means follower has entries from an old term
Leader can find where that term ends in its log

Jump directly to that boundary instead of linear scan This is analogous to TCP Selective Acknowledgment (SACK): instead of acknowledging "next expected byte", receiver says "I have bytes X-Y, tell me what comes after Y".

div.p: Total: 501 round trips Each rejection decrements nextIndex by only 1, requiring linear scan backward through divergent region.
Total: 2 round trips ConflictTerm enables jump directly to divergence point by searching leader's log for matching term boundary.

text.tspan: Cmd: A B C ... Z X ... Y
Cmd: A B C ... Z D ... E
Cmd: A B C ... Z X ... Y
Cmd: A B C ... Z D ... E
// Find first index with this term
// On receiving rejection with conflict info
// Search for ConflictTerm in our log

div.p: Find last index where term == 1 → Found at index 500
